meta.title: FARMER DROP — Knowledge Graph
meta.generated_note: Nodes/edges extracted from the Farmer Drop knowledge map (conceptual §2, business §3, token §4, drop-score §5, data/routing §6, GTM §7). Terminology normalized against Glossary__ASR_uncertainties.md. Source is voice-note transcripts (whisper ggml-tiny); every ⚠ token is unverified. Code lives in Volkmar's repo, not here — this is the spec/knowledge layer.
decision_rules_applied: DR-1 (take %): platform-take node carries confidence:unresolved — 0.10% vs 10% is a 100x ambiguity that breaks the $5.2B valuation; model halts until resolved.
DR-2 (credit boost): credit-boost node modeled as a COST line with unattributed funding source (breakage / 0.25% wedge / token float).
DR-3 (token count): all token-layer nodes carry confidence:verify — blocked pending one-token-or-two decision + regulatory classification; no chain code.
DR-4 (terminology): 'HostDrop' used globally; 'a.k.a. Drop Spot' noted in description only.
DR-5 (legal fidelity): any glossary ⚠ item blocks downstream document generation until confirmed.

nodes.label: Member
failure_modes: Member acquisition (not tech) is the binding constraint on the scale story (§3.4.6).

nodes.label: Farmer
failure_modes: Wants diversified income without commodity lock-in; 80% claim unproven until a real unit-economics sheet exists (§3.2).

nodes.label: HostDrop
failure_modes: Drop-density thresholds unmodeled — density, not throughput, gates the network (§3.4.6).
Terminology drift: a.k.a. 'Drop Spot'; DR-4 fixes 'HostDrop' globally.

nodes.label: Independent Contractor

metrics.commission: 100% commission = 3% of drop volume for N months (out of platform's 10%)

failure_modes: Recruits/trains HostDrops and links farmers to members; N-month window and take source depend on the unresolved platform take-% (DR-1).

nodes.label: Driver / Resource-Holder
failure_modes: Anyone with a truck fills backhaul routes; supply of idle capacity and route reliability unproven at scale.

nodes.label: Platform (Farmer Drop)
failure_modes: Decentralized-Amazon coordination layer; app code held by Volkmar, not in this repo — integration target with enumerated unknowns (BRIEF §1).

nodes.label: Drop Score
failure_modes: Factor tree (5 domains, 19 factors, 38 evaluable indicators) now specced in drop_score_graph.json — weights and scoring rubric still deliberately undefined.
Presented as a guidance tree, not a black box — the tree spec now exists; the rubric that scores it does not.

nodes.label: Per-Transaction Surcharge $0.55
failure_modes: Over-covers so small baskets don't leave the platform underwater; framed as transparent but the buffer margin is not itself modeled.

nodes.label: Stripe Fixed + % Cost
failure_modes: Double-charging risk (§3.4.3): platform adds 2.9% AND passes Stripe's 2.9% — confirm it is not both eating and charging the processor fee.
Token rail (Phase 1/2) is the only mechanism that actually removes this cost — PMA status does not (§3.4.4).

nodes.label: Cart Surcharge 3.15%
failure_modes: Double-charging risk (§3.4.3): the 2.9% component may stack with the passed-through Stripe 2.9%; make the stacking explicit.

nodes.label: 0.25% Strategic Wedge
failure_modes: Is the candidate funding source for the negative-margin credit boost (§3.4.2) — but 0.25% cannot obviously fund a 2% boost; show the math.

nodes.label: Platform Take — 10% vs 0.10% ⚠

metrics.working_analysis: supports 7–12% gross, centered 10% — see linked templates

failure_modes: LOAD-BEARING AMBIGUITY (DR-1): stated as '0.10%' but the valuation only closes at ~10%. 0.10% of $100M/wk = $100k/wk, which cannot produce $5M/wk net or the $5.2B valuation.
Working analysis (take_rate_math.html) refutes 0.10% decisively and offers 3 template solutions — but the final number still needs Magnus/Volkmar confirmation; the model HALTS until then.
Credit boost (2%) and contractor cut (3%) are both carved out of this number — every downstream figure inherits the ambiguity.

nodes.label: Credit Purchase Boost ~2%
failure_modes: COST LINE (DR-2): giving 2% more credits than dollars received is a direct 2% loss unless funded by breakage, the 0.25% wedge, or token/float seigniorage.
Funding source is unattributed — silently erodes the platform take until made explicit.
'$100 -> $200' vs '$100 -> $102' must be resolved (glossary ⚠).

nodes.label: Contractor Cut 3%
failure_modes: Carved out of the platform take — if the take is really 0.10% (DR-1) this 3% is mathematically impossible.

nodes.label: Decentralized Distribution Economics (80% / 5% per hop)

metrics.claim: beats incumbent even with two hops

failure_modes: Two-hop haircut math needs a worked example (§3.4.5): '80% - 5% - 5% beats incumbent' depends entirely on the incumbent baseline (grocery/distributor take often 30-50%).
Incumbent baseline % is an unresolved open question — put both columns side by side before asserting the wedge.

nodes.label: Scale Scenario — $100M/wk -> $5.2B
failure_modes: Entire valuation assumes ~10% take — collapses under the DR-1 0.10% reading (§3.4.1).
Throughput story, but adoption is the real constraint (§3.4.6): model CAC and drop-density thresholds, not just steady-state throughput.

nodes.label: Fee-as-Tax / PMA Framing

metrics.claim: platform + HostDrops are PMAs -> some fees are collectively-avoidable 'taxes'

failure_modes: Legal-characterization claim, not a mechanism (§3.4.4): processor/network fees are contractual costs, not taxes; PMA status does not make Stripe's 2.9% disappear.
Separate actual cost reduction (token rail replacing card rail) from tax positioning — the latter invites regulatory scrutiny.

nodes.label: Churches Distribution
failure_modes: Congregation-scale bulk onboarding is the core member-acquisition bet; CAC unmodeled.

nodes.label: Conferences
failure_modes: Second acquisition channel; conversion and cost unquantified.

nodes.label: Test App as Sales Tool

metrics.mechanism: influential testers make Stripe purchases with no money withdrawn, walk the flow, then vouch

failure_modes: Diverse testers surface UX edge cases; depends on Volkmar's app reaching demo-ready / 100% test coverage (§8).

nodes.label: Mid-August Launch Deadline

metrics.framing: 'launch by mid-August or lose the race'

failure_modes: Schedule pressure driving the whole build; blocking items (take-%, tokenomics) are unresolved against this clock.

nodes.label: Regenerative Legislation
failure_modes: Policy/funding hook for Drop Score; dependency on legislative timing and actual fund availability unverified.

nodes.label: Honey Coin (asset-backed — deferred)

metrics.role: ASSET-BACKED loyalty token — explicitly NOT the first deploy (user-confirmed 2026-07-04)

failure_modes: DR-3 RESOLVED 2026-07-04: two-token architecture — a transfer-efficiency coin deploys FIRST; Honey Coin comes later, asset-backed, via the compadres template.
Regulatory (§4): a USD-redeemable, USD-boosted, food-spendable token is near stored-value / money-transmission / possibly-security territory — needs counsel BEFORE launch.
Not ready to launch; asset-backing spec (reserves, custody, on/off-ramp) still required.
nodes.link_label: Rails: Stripe fee structure vs chain coin

nodes.label: Phase 0 — Fiat + Credits
failure_modes: Current state; ledger design lives in Volkmar's '.7 backend' — scope unconfirmed (§8).

nodes.label: Phase 1 — Alt Transfer (drop Stripe fees)

metrics.mechanism: drop Stripe fees for anyone accepting an alternative transfer form — realized as the CPDR-style transfer coin (first deploy)

failure_modes: This is the FIRST real cost reduction — the only mechanism that genuinely removes card-rail cost (contrast with PMA tax framing, §3.4.4).
nodes.link_label: Rails: Stripe fee structure vs chain coin

nodes.label: compadres repo · Base chain

metrics.repo: compadres — CONFIRMED at ~/GitHub/compadres: CPDR low-extraction token/exchange test harness (TransferRouter, mock USDC, AMM, burn/redeem, fee measurement)

failure_modes: Repo confirmed (2026-07-04); asset-backed layer deliberately deferred behind docs/06_asset_backed_template.md + IReserveAdapter.sol. No production chain launch until regulatory classification is resolved.

nodes.label: Transfer Coin (first deploy — CPDR)

metrics.status: user-confirmed 2026-07-04: the FIRST coin — efficient P2P transfers between people, replacing expensive Stripe rails
metrics.harness: compadres CPDR: TransferRouter, fee measurement, reconciliation

failure_modes: DR-3 resolved: this is token #1 of two (Honey Coin, asset-backed, comes later).
Still requires regulatory review before production launch; compadres v0 is a test harness, not a deployed product.
nodes.link_label: Rails: Stripe fee structure vs chain coin

nodes.label: Distribution / Routing Engine

metrics.objective: optimize routing (backhaul chaining TX->Gulf->FL) + production allocation
metrics.future: eventually own trucks as a sub-service

failure_modes: Instrument-for-data FIRST — do not over-engineer the math before there is data to scale models on (§6).

nodes.label: Demand + Supply / Seasonality Data
failure_modes: Data schema for demand/supply/seasonality is the prerequisite; without instrumentation the routing optimization is premature (§6, BRIEF §2.5).

nodes.label: Backhaul Chaining

metrics.principle: monetize idle private capacity near term

failure_modes: Depends on driver idle-capacity supply and reliable route matching — unproven at network scale.

nodes.label: Seasonal Prediction (peach-tree test case)
failure_modes: Concrete first prediction target; success depends on demand/supply instrumentation existing first (no premature optimization, §6).

edges.relation: pays 3% of drop volume / N months
farmer nets more after USD conversion
